response:
The error occurs because your project is attempting to load WPF assemblies that are not available in the .NET 7 runtime on Windows without explicit configuration.

You need to ensure that your WPF library project targets .NET 7 or .NET 8 instead of the older .NET Framework 4.x to maintain compatibility with your main application.

cousin projects often face this issue when mixing legacy frameworks with modern cross-platform runtimes, but updating the target framework resolves the mismatch.

Configure your WPF project file to include the necessary package references for WPF under .NET Core by adding the Microsoft.NETCore.App dependency or using the official WPF SDK.

Once the target framework is aligned and the correct packages are restored, the assembly loading error will disappear and your application will run smoothly on both Windows and Mac.
